fresh_facts.claim: USS Tripoli (LHA-7) and the 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit, carrying approximately 3,500 sailors and Marines, entered CENTCOM area of operations on March 27, 2026.
USS Boxer ARG/MEU with approximately 2,500 Marines deployed from San Diego on March 18, 2026, en route to relieve USS Tripoli.
The Pentagon was considering deploying 3,000-4,000 soldiers from the 82nd Airborne Division and up to 10,000 additional ground troops for Middle East operations as of March 24-27, 2026.
Thirteen US troops were killed and 290 wounded since the start of the 2026 Iran war on February 28.
US and Israeli joint strikes on Iran occurred on February 28, 2026, marking the beginning of the 2026 Iran war.
USS Gerald R. Ford was en route to the Middle East as of February 13, 2026, as part of US military reinforcements.
Approximately 50,000 US troops were deployed to the Middle East prior to the March 2026 reinforcements.
The US expanded military operations against Iranian missile and naval threats as of March 20-27, 2026.
The US has struck approximately 9,000 Iranian targets, focusing on missiles, naval assets, and infrastructure.
Pentagon released footage of strikes on March 27, 2026.
President Trump paused power grid strikes pending talks as of late March 2026, while Iran denied direct negotiations.
Iran continues missile and drone attacks on Israel and US positions as of March 28, 2026.
Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi rejected US negotiation claims on March 25, 2026, calling them 'fake news' and admissions of defeat.
Iranian military spokesman Ebrahim Zolfaghari denied direct talks with the US on March 25-26, 2026, despite US reports of indirect messages via mediators.
President Trump claimed progress in talks and reported a 15-point ceasefire plan delivered via Pakistan on March 24-25, 2026.
Iran insisted on a full war end rather than partial ceasefire deals as of March 25-26, 2026.
An Iranian official signaled a diplomatic opening on March 26, 2026, citing US 'realism' while indirect nuclear restraint talks continue.
Pakistan, Egypt, and Gulf states acted as mediators in indirect US-Iran communications as of March 25-26, 2026, with Pakistan offering to host preliminary talks.
Qatar ruled out involvement in US-Iran mediation efforts on March 25, 2026.
Iran threatened to target Kharg Island amid ongoing war tensions as of late March 2026.
The 2026 Iran war is linked to 2025-2026 protests and crackdowns within Iran that killed thousands, according to US attributions.

regime_fall.current_status: Massive U.S. and Israeli strikes on March 28 targeted Iran's nuclear facilities, military infrastructure, and industrial sites across multiple provinces, with approximately 9,000 targets struck and operations nearing completion within weeks. The military-dominated regime under new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei continues rejecting ceasefire terms and maintaining offensive capabilities despite intensive air campaign, while U.S. military presence expands with 50,000+ troops deployed and additional reinforcements en route.
regime_fall.last_major_event: U.S. and Israeli coordinated air campaign targeting approximately 9,000 Iranian military, nuclear, and infrastructure targets reached peak intensity on March 28, 2026, with Pentagon indicating operations nearing completion within weeks as ground force reinforcements deploy to theater.
